key_insight: PhononScore is a fast, structure-based scoring function that predicts dynamical stability and reranks generated crystals without expensive explicit phonon calculations.
engineering_relevance: It enables second-level stability screening for materials discovery workflows, making it practical for high-throughput generation, active learning, and reinforcement learning loops.
method: Trains on a multi-fidelity phonon dataset of 157,463 crystal structures.
Learns a unified stability score directly from crystal structures.
Provides a phonon-aware reranking signal for candidate pools from multiple crystal generators.
evidence_signal: On PhononBench, the average dynamical stability rate of candidate pools rose from 30.7% to 83.7%, with Top-10 candidates reaching 97.5%; on the DFT-PBE benchmark, PhononScore-DFT reached 93.0% Top-100 stability.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of the training data, so generalization to unfamiliar chemistries or synthesis-relevant constraints still needs careful validation.
paper_title: PhononScore: a phonon-aware scoring function for dynamical stability
